How We Earn Money
Methodology

Our Research Methodology

Every review and rating on CreditDoc follows a rigorous, repeatable research process. Here's exactly how we evaluate the companies featured on our site.

Our 5-Factor Rating System

Every company listed on CreditDoc is evaluated across five core factors. Each factor is scored on a scale of 1.0 to 5.0, and the overall rating is an equally weighted average of all five scores.

1

Value

Is the service worth the price? We evaluate monthly fees, setup costs, money-back guarantees, and the overall cost-to-benefit ratio. We compare pricing against the breadth and quality of services offered, and consider whether lower-priced alternatives deliver comparable results.

2

Effectiveness

Does the company deliver results? We analyze publicly available outcomes data, user-reported score improvements, CFPB complaint volumes relative to company size, and the range of dispute strategies employed (goodwill letters, debt validation, cease-and-desist letters, etc.).

3

Customer Service

How responsive and helpful is the company? We evaluate availability of support channels (phone, email, chat), whether clients are assigned a dedicated advisor, response times based on user reports, and overall customer satisfaction as reflected in third-party reviews.

4

Transparency

Is the company upfront about pricing, processes, and realistic expectations? We evaluate whether pricing is clearly displayed on the website, whether the company makes unrealistic promises (such as guaranteeing specific score increases), and the clarity of their contracts and terms of service.

5

Ease of Use

How easy is it to get started and stay informed? We evaluate the sign-up process, whether the company offers a mobile app or online portal, the quality of progress tracking tools, and overall user experience based on public reviews.

Our Data Sources

We cross-reference multiple independent data sources to form a comprehensive picture of each company. No single source determines our rating.

Better Business Bureau (BBB)

Accreditation status, letter grades, complaint history, and resolution track record.

CFPB Complaint Database

Consumer complaints filed with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, including company responses.

User Reviews

Verified customer reviews from Trustpilot, Google, BBB, and other platforms, analyzed for common themes.

Direct Company Research

We visit company websites directly to verify pricing, features, service tiers, and terms of service.

Pricing Verification

Monthly checks of all listed prices against current company websites to ensure accuracy.

Regulatory Records

State attorney general actions, FTC enforcement actions, and court records when available.

Our Review Process

Each company listing goes through a structured review pipeline before publication. Here's how it works:

1

Initial Research

We identify the company and gather baseline information: founding date, headquarters, services offered, target market, and key differentiators.

2

Data Collection

We pull data from all sources listed above: BBB, CFPB, user reviews, direct pricing verification, and regulatory records.

3

Expert Analysis

Our team applies the 5-factor rating system, writes the detailed review narrative, identifies pros/cons, and determines "best for" categories.

4

Peer Review

Ratings and review content are cross-checked for accuracy, consistency with our methodology, and fairness.

5

Publication

The review is published on CreditDoc with a "last updated" date. All reviews include our editorial diagnosis -- a plain-language summary of who the service is best for and what to watch out for.

6

Ongoing Monitoring

All listings are reviewed quarterly. Pricing is verified monthly. If a company's BBB rating changes, receives regulatory action, or makes significant changes to their services, we update our review accordingly.

Update Frequency

What We Check Frequency
Pricing and service tiers Monthly
Full review and ratings Quarterly
BBB ratings and accreditation Quarterly
CFPB complaint data Quarterly
Regulatory actions or legal issues As they occur
Company closures or acquisitions As they occur

Editorial Independence

CreditDoc earns revenue through affiliate partnerships with some of the companies listed on our site. When you click certain links and sign up for a service, we may receive a referral fee. This is how we fund our research and keep the site free for consumers.

However, compensation never influences our editorial content. Our ratings, rankings, and recommendations are determined solely by our research methodology described above. We frequently feature and recommend companies that do not have an affiliate relationship with us.

If you believe any of our reviews contain inaccurate information, we encourage you to contact us at gian.eao@gmail.com. We take corrections seriously and will investigate promptly. For our full affiliate disclosure, visit our Disclosure page.

Rating Scale

5.0

Outstanding

Exceptional across all five evaluation criteria. Industry-leading in multiple areas.

4.0

Excellent

Strong performance overall with minor areas for improvement. Recommended.

3.0

Good

Solid service with notable strengths and weaknesses. Worth considering depending on your priorities.

2.0

Below Average

Significant concerns in one or more areas. Proceed with caution and consider alternatives.

1.0

Poor

Serious issues identified. Significant red flags, regulatory concerns, or consistent negative consumer reports.

Suggest a Correction

We strive for accuracy in everything we publish, but we're not infallible. If you notice incorrect pricing, outdated information, a factual error, or believe a rating doesn't reflect the current state of a company, please let us know.

Email us at gian.eao@gmail.com with the subject line "Correction Request" and include:

  • The page URL or company name
  • What information you believe is incorrect
  • The correct information (with a source, if possible)

We review all correction requests within 5 business days and will update our content if warranted.
